prompt: can you write up what happened? We just finished debugging a Rails migration failure in staging. The migration added a NOT NULL constraint to the users.confirmed_at column but existing rows had null values, so it failed with 'PG::NotNullViolation'. We tried rolling back but the rollback also failed because of a dependent index. Eventually we wrote a backfill script to set confirmed_at = created_at for all null rows, then re-ran the migration — it succeeded. This was in staging only, no users affected. Took about 45 minutes.
assertions: Output file is a .md file in the outputs directory
Document contains a ## Root Cause section
Document contains an ## Impact section (or equivalent coverage of impact)
Document contains a ## Resolution section with the specific SQL commands used
Document contains an ## Action Items section with at least one checklist item
Document contains a ## Lessons Learned section
Severity field uses the defined scale (Critical, High, Medium, or Low) and is Medium or Low (staging only, no users affected)
Root cause explains why the migration failed — the null assumption and why it wasn't caught
